source: D93257 Halcyon Evolve User Assistance Plan Rev02.docx.pdf
title: 1. Introduction > 1.2 Purpose and Scope of the UAP
text_content: The purpose of this UAP is to: Identify deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, content strategy and information architecture, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective documentation for users of the product. Serve as a communication vehicle for the project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. User Assistance Developers (UADs) are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up to date throughout the project and updated as needed. If key milestones remain unchanged, details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. Scope of UA updates is set by the trigger for the release (section 1.1).  UA included in this release is listed at section 2. For details on the UA to be created or updated for this release, see section 5, Detailed Project Scope.

source: D54550 HAL 4.0 Validation Plan Rev 05.docx.pdf
title: 2. Validation Overview > 2.2.4 Installation/Upgrade Testing
text_content: As part of Validation TRR (merge Quote to Cash (Q2C) activities), Product Life Cycle Services (PLCS) will work with Varian Installation Team to perform validation testing of the HAL v4.0 associated Service Technical Instructions (STI’s).  The STI’s are the Installation and Service documentation required to install, upgrade, and maintain the new SW and HW for the device under test. PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. PLCS will use engineering systems and, if available, non-clinical systems to develop the Service Technical Instructions (STI’s) and to perform the validation testing.  Perform installation and upgrades as the User. text_content: device to the hospital local-area-network (LAN) is controlled to limit access from the network to only trusted applications and systems. As appropriate, the use of firewall devices may be supplemented for additional protection and support in blocking unauthorized access. Lastly, users are directed in the user documentation to follow the designated security procedures for properly shutting down the software system, which may include, but are not limited to logging out of the active user account, logging out of the application software, shutting down the device workstation, physically locking the control console, and physically locking the device equipment. These measures provide further protection against unauthorized access and use when the device is left unattended.
